caption: The video appears to be an educational tutorial on cryogenic engineering, specifically focusing on the work required per unit mass of gas liquefied. The presentation slide shown is part of Tutorial 4 and includes calculations related to this topic. Initially, it displays the formula for work per unit mass of gas liquefied, with given values such as \( \frac{W_c}{\dot{m}} = 486.2 \) and \( y = 0.0215 \). It then proceeds to calculate \( \frac{W_c}{\dot{m}_l} \), which results in approximately 22613.95 J/g. As the video progresses, a new bullet point titled "Figure of Merit (FOM)" appears, suggesting that the next segment will delve into this concept, likely explaining its significance in evaluating the efficiency or performance of the liquefaction process. The tutorial is presented by Prof. M D Atrey from the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Bombay, indicating a high level of academic rigor and expertise in the field.
